message:
br-bd-fartk extend fuzz_mvcc_operations to drive advanced commit paths

The previous harness only exercised the generic MvccStore::commit path
and plain stage_write. The adaptive conflict policy (Strict/SafeMerge/
Adaptive with expected-loss decision model) — which the README calls
out as a signature differentiator — runs through commit_ssi,
commit_fcw_prechecked, stage_write_with_proof, and effective_policy.
None of those were fuzzed.

New coverage (10 opcodes, size-bounded to 2048 bytes for > 500 exec/s):
- begin / stage_write (as before)
- stage_write_with_proof with MergeProof::{Unsafe, DisjointBlocks}
  (variants well-defined on arbitrary payloads; the byte-range proof
  forms are tested by dedicated unit tests)
- commit / commit_ssi / commit_fcw_prechecked — all three routes hit
- set_conflict_policy(Strict|SafeMerge|Adaptive) mid-stream
- pre-commit snapshot capture with oracle-frozen expected state
- abort / prune_safe

Invariants:
- I1: strictly monotonic commit sequences (hard assert).
- I2: snapshot isolation — a snapshot captured at time T must never
  observe bytes committed after T. Frozen oracle state at capture time
  is the expected read.
- I3: post-commit visibility — the oracle's most recent committed
  bytes are observable at the current snapshot (unless pruned).
- I4: conflict_policy enum shape + effective_policy resolves Adaptive
  into Strict or SafeMerge.

Seed corpus adds seed_policy_commit_paths (26 bytes) that drives a
sequence through commit_ssi + commit_fcw_prechecked + policy toggles
so the new opcodes are reached from the first fuzz iteration. 200-run
smoke confirms zero crashes and coverage 3193 features / 9561 ft.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
